newsSince: No new material company-specific news since June 9. The $105M+ multi-system orders from a tier-1 OSAT and a leading HBM maker (Hawk systems, 2027 delivery) were announced June 2, 2026, ahead of the window and already captured in the June 9 verdict. Stock essentially flat (+1.9%) since.

verdicts.rankRationale: AI-node bottleneck lens: Hawk metrology for HBM/2.5D-3D OSAT is a genuine packaging chokepoint, but the $105M order (Jun 2) is pre-window and priced. No fresh evidence since June 9; flat tape. Hold rank 3 among picks-and-shovels WFE.
verdicts.bull: $105M HBM/OSAT 2027 backlog confirms Hawk is designed-in at advanced-packaging chokepoints; smaller-cap leverage to AI attach.
verdicts.neutral: Strong order but already announced and largely priced; awaiting next datapoint.
verdicts.bear: Advanced-packaging capex is cyclical and customer-concentrated; +48% YTD leaves less margin of safety.

verdicts.rankRationale: CPO/chiplet-complexity lens: top inspection beneficiary of advanced-packaging and chiplet complexity. Post-Jun 2 spike has settled; no new evidence in-window so crowding/asymmetry argument from June 9 stands. Hold rank 1.
verdicts.bull: Best-positioned small-cap inspection name as chiplet/CPO complexity raises metrology intensity per package.
verdicts.neutral: Order spike has been digested; thesis intact but no fresh catalyst since June 9.
verdicts.bear: Rank 1 leaves no headroom; cyclical packaging capex could compress orders.

verdicts.rankRationale: Under-owned-bottleneck master list: advanced-packaging & substrate scarcity exposure remains strong, but at rank 2 there is little headroom and the supporting $105M order predates the window. Nothing since June 9 justifies displacing the top name. Hold.
verdicts.bull: Scarce, designed-in exposure to CoWoS/HBM-attach inspection - a real packaging bottleneck.
verdicts.neutral: Already richly ranked; June 2 order book supports but does not extend the thesis.
verdicts.bear: Cyclical packaging capex; rerating after +48% YTD limits incremental alpha.
